documents.title: Tooling API Overview
documents.text: The Tooling API exposes metadata used by developer tooling. Authenticate with OAuth using your org login endpoint, then send REST requests to /services/data/vXX.X/tooling/. Use OAuth scopes that permit API access and include an Authorization Bearer token on each request.

documents.title: Tooling API Authentication
documents.text: Use OAuth 2.0 to obtain an access token. Supported flows include web server, user-agent, JWT bearer, and refresh token. Include Authorization: Bearer <token> and use the instance URL returned by login.

documents.title: SOQL Relationship Queries
documents.text: Relationship queries traverse parent-to-child and child-to-parent records. Use dot notation for parent fields and nested SELECT for child relationships.

documents.title: sf project deploy start
documents.text: Use sf project deploy start to deploy metadata from your local project to an org. Common flags include --target-org, --manifest, and --test-level.

documents.title: ContentVersion Object
documents.text: ContentVersion represents a specific version of a Salesforce file. Key fields include ContentDocumentId, VersionData, PathOnClient, and IsLatest.
